_metadata.description: make_AGENTS skill — generates tool-agnostic AGENTS.md project context files. Sibling to make_agent.md and make_gem.md.
_metadata.instructions: MD = the why (mission, principles, narrative). JSON = the what (required/optional sections, template strings, compact boilerplate, validation). Update both files together.

skill_details.description: Generates AGENTS.md project context files that are tool-agnostic and embed the behavioral discipline.

agent_type.description: Workflow-based skill: identify project context → generate AGENTS.md → propose write → wait for confirmation → write file.

behavioral_discipline._description: make_AGENTS itself operates under the behavioral discipline. Source: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md/.json.

_skipped_principles_rationale.P-005: Single-step workflow (one AGENTS.md per invocation), no decomposition needed.

inputs.name: project_root
inputs.format: Absolute or relative path to the project's root directory

inputs.name: existing_claude_md
inputs.format: Path to an existing CLAUDE.md to migrate from (optional)

outputs.name: agents_md
outputs.format: Generated AGENTS.md written at project root
outputs.conditions: Created if absent, or proposed-as-update if present (per P-002 — never silently overwrites)

outputs.name: discipline_files_packaged
outputs.format: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md + knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json copied into the TARGET project's knowledge/ folder (with snapshot header indicating origin commit-sha)
outputs.conditions: Required when the target repo does not already have these files (either at canonical knowledge/ path OR at a cloned-subdirectory path like make_ai_agents/knowledge/). Skipped if already present; the Working Style pointer is then updated to whichever local path resolves. Without this packaging step, the Working Style pointer is a broken reference.

outputs.name: handoff_repo_co_located
outputs.format: handoff/ clone at target repo root + handoff/ entry added to the target's .gitignore
outputs.conditions: CONDITIONAL — required when the target repo participates in the cross-repo handoff convention. Participation detected via (i) auto-signal: target repo has a handoffs/ folder; OR (ii) explicit user confirmation to the prompt 'Will this agent communicate with other agents via handoffs?'. Skipped otherwise — the recognition snippet still ships standalone in Working Style. Added 2026-05-28 (Sprint F).

io_contract.side_effects: Creates or updates AGENTS.md at project root after user confirmation. Copies knowledge/behavioral_discipline.{md,json} into the target's knowledge/ folder unless already present (with snapshot header for traceability). Does NOT delete the existing CLAUDE.md — that is a separate follow-up step.

agents_md_required_sections._description: Sections that EVERY generated AGENTS.md must contain, in this order. Used by make_AGENTS to generate and by validation to check.

sections.heading: # <Project Name>
sections.description: H1 with project name + one-line description below. Tag line, not paragraph.

sections.heading: ## Project Purpose
sections.description: What the repo IS (3-5 bullets) and what it is NOT (2-4 bullets). Includes audience.

sections.heading: ## Structure
sections.description: Annotated folder layout in a code block. Reference, not exhaustive — top-level + key subfolders only.

sections.heading: ## Working Style
sections.description: Behavioral discipline pointer (substituted from compact_boilerplate.working_style_template), any project-specific rules, AND the canonical handoff recognition section baked from handoff/AGENTS_snippet.md (per Quickstart step 4.5 / AGENTS-QC-008).
must_include: discipline pointer to knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md or equivalent

handoff recognition section (## Handoff document recognition) baked verbatim from handoff/AGENTS_snippet.md — heading + path-pattern table + 7 numbered rules + status/direction enum quick-lookups. Verified by AGENTS-QC-008.

sections.heading: ## Active Context
sections.description: Current state, in-flight work, recent major changes, open issues. Date-stamped.
must_include: _Last updated: YYYY-MM-DD_ line at the top of the section

agents_md_optional_sections._description: Sections to include only when the inclusion criteria apply.

sections.heading: ## Domain Terms
sections.include_when: The project has non-obvious vocabulary that an LLM would interpret wrong without explicit definitions.

sections.heading: ## External System Lessons
sections.include_when: The project interacts with external systems (Canvas API, GitHub API, Stripe, etc.) with non-obvious behaviors discovered through use.
sections.format: Per-system entry: Behavior / Why it matters / How to handle

sections.heading: ## Migration Notes
sections.include_when: The project is mid-migration (CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md, library swap, framework upgrade). Remove section once migration completes.

sections.heading: ## Existing Tooling
sections.include_when: The project has scripts or utilities the LLM should reuse before generating new code.
sections.format: Tool / Purpose / When to use table

sections.heading: ## Knowledge Index
sections.include_when: The project has a folder of topic-specific knowledge files (e.g., `knowledge/`, `.claude/knowledge/`, `lib/agents/knowledge/`) that the LLM loads on demand per task focus, rather than as a stable embedded prefix on every call. The index is anti-bloat — it lets the AGENTS.md stay lean while pointing agents at the relevant deep context per-task.
sections.format: Topic | File path | When to load — table. One row per knowledge file. The 'When to load' column is the trigger that tells the LLM which file is relevant to a given task; without explicit triggers the LLM either loads everything (bloat) or nothing (incomplete). Added as an optional section 2026-05-13 after the m119-master GG case showed this anti-bloat pattern in active use without a canonical home in make_AGENTS.
sections.example:
| Topic | File | When to load |
|---|---|---|
| Converter behavior | `.claude/knowledge/converter.md` | When working on RMarkdown→Quarto conversion |
| Canvas URL handling | `.claude/knowledge/urls-canvas.md` | When debugging Canvas URL/redirect logic |

agents_md_template._description: The skeleton AGENTS.md the make_AGENTS skill substitutes into. Placeholders use {{double_braces}}.

compact_boilerplate._description: Templates substituted into the generated AGENTS.md. The discipline pointer is the most important — it propagates the behavioral discipline from this repo into every project that uses an AGENTS.md generated by this skill.
compact_boilerplate.working_style_template:
This project follows the behavioral discipline defined in `knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md` (if present in this repo) or the equivalent discipline loaded via the host tool's skill system. **Read that file before doing non-trivial work in this repo.**

The four no-override principles — **P-001 Read Before Claiming, P-003 Stop on Defect, P-007 Pull Don't Push, P-010 Respect Intent** — apply unconditionally; the other six (P-002, P-004, P-005, P-006, P-008, P-009) have documented override conditions in the discipline file.
compact_boilerplate.handoff_recognition_instructions: Always baked into every generated AGENTS.md per Quickstart step 4.5. Read handoff/AGENTS_snippet.md from this Make-AI-Agents repo (the handoff convention repo is cloned at handoff/). Copy its content from '## Handoff document recognition' through end-of-file VERBATIM into the generated AGENTS.md's Working Style section (after the discipline pointer subsection, before Active Context). Prepend two HTML-comment refresh-trace lines above the section: '<!-- handoff/AGENTS_snippet.md @ <sha> -->' and '<!-- refresh: cd handoff && git pull -->' so a future reader sees when it was baked and how to refresh. The snippet is SELF-CONTAINED — its 7 rules + path-pattern table + status/direction enums do NOT depend on the target repo having handoff/ co-located at runtime. Co-location of the handoff/ clone is a SEPARATE conditional step (see Quickstart 4.5 (b)) triggered by participation signals (target has handoffs/ folder, or user confirms 'will this agent communicate with other agents'). AGENTS-QC-008 verifies both the baked snippet and the conditional co-location.

pre_run_checklist: project_root exists and is a directory
knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md is readable (or equivalent discipline source declared)
If existing_claude_md is provided, it is readable
post_run_checklist: All required sections present in order: title, project_purpose, structure, working_style, active_context
Working Style section contains a pointer to the behavioral discipline
Active Context section starts with a `_Last updated: YYYY-MM-DD_` line
No tool-specific language in the file body (no 'Claude Code does X' style instructions)

Refresh only: no stale vendoring-pattern tokens (`git subtree`, `git subtree pull/push`, `subtree add`, `.gitmodules`, `git submodule`) describe the CURRENT or RECOMMENDED posture in any section (AGENTS-QC-007). Negative-framing or historical-migration mentions are allowed.

Working Style contains the canonical handoff recognition section (AGENTS-QC-008): heading `## Handoff document recognition` present + path-pattern table present (with `Path pattern` / `What it is` columns and rows for `handoffs/HANDOFF_<topic>.md`, `handoffs/<YYYY-MM-DD>_<topic>.md`, etc.) + all 7 numbered rules present + `## Quick lookup — Status enum` and `## Quick lookup — Direction enum` tables present. Conditional: if the target repo has a `handoffs/` folder, the `handoff/` clone is co-located at the target repo root AND `handoff/` is listed in the target's `.gitignore`.
success_criteria: Generated AGENTS.md is tool-agnostic (readable by any agentic dev tool)
Behavioral discipline embed present in Working Style
Each optional section present has its inclusion criteria met

test_cases.name: Generate AGENTS.md for a fresh project
test_cases.expected_output: AGENTS.md created with all 5 required sections, no optional sections, discipline pointer in Working Style.

test_cases.name: Generate AGENTS.md for a project with existing CLAUDE.md
test_cases.expected_output: AGENTS.md created. Workspace rules from CLAUDE.md migrated to project_purpose + working_style. Migration Notes section included documenting CLAUDE.md deletion as a follow-up step.

test_cases.name: Reject silently overwriting an existing AGENTS.md
test_cases.expected_output: Per P-002: skill proposes the new AGENTS.md as a diff against the existing file, waits for confirmation. Does not silently overwrite.

test_cases.name: Refresh: stale subtree language is rewritten, not preserved
test_cases.input: project_root=/path with an existing AGENTS.md whose Active Context bullet describes `make_ai_agents/` as a `git subtree` (or any other vendoring-pattern token from AGENTS-QC-007.trigger_tokens)
test_cases.expected_output: Skill detects the trigger token, proposes a rewrite to the canonical clone+gitignore bullet (template in make_AGENTS.md → Common Pitfall #1), and surfaces each rewrite in the refresh handoff doc so the maintainer reviews each change. Does NOT preserve the stale language under P-007 — the language IS the defect, not adjacent code.

qc_checks._description: Skill-specific validation checks. These are NOT the BD-QC checks (those live in knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json) — these are checks specific to whether the GENERATED AGENTS.md meets the contract.

checks.name: All required sections present
checks.rule: Generated AGENTS.md contains all 5 required sections from agents_md_required_sections in the specified order.

checks.name: Working Style contains discipline pointer
checks.rule: Working Style section references `knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md` or an equivalent discipline source. Without this, the discipline doesn't propagate from this repo to the project.

checks.name: Active Context has date stamp
checks.rule: Active Context section starts with `_Last updated: YYYY-MM-DD_`. The date stamp signals freshness; without it, stale context goes undetected.

checks.name: No tool-specific language
checks.rule: Generated AGENTS.md body does not contain tool-specific instructions (e.g., 'When using Claude Code...', 'In Cursor only...'). Tool-specific guidance belongs in tool-specific config files, not AGENTS.md.

checks.name: Optional sections meet inclusion criteria
checks.rule: If an optional section is present, the project must satisfy that section's `include_when` criterion in agents_md_optional_sections. Empty optional sections (e.g., a Domain Terms table with placeholder rows) fail this check.

checks.name: Discipline files co-located with AGENTS.md
checks.rule: The Working Style section's discipline pointer must resolve to a file that actually exists in the target repo. PASS conditions (any one): (a) `<target_repo>/knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md` and `behavioral_discipline.json` both exist at the canonical path; (b) the AGENTS.md pointer references a cloned-subdirectory path (e.g., `make_ai_agents/knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md`) AND that path resolves to a file — NOTE: only a FULL Make-AI-Agents clone (or another repo explicitly carrying `knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md` at a known relative path) satisfies this; single-skill clones (gh_issues_agent/, canvas-toolbox/, etc.) do NOT carry the discipline by default — verify the file actually resolves at the path before claiming condition (b); (c) the host tool's skill system loads the equivalent discipline at runtime (declare this explicitly in Working Style if relying on this path). FAIL: the pointer references a path that doesn't resolve. Without local discipline files, the pointer is a broken reference and the discipline does not propagate to agents working in this repo. Severity critical because the entire purpose of make_AGENTS is to propagate the discipline — a broken pointer defeats the skill.

checks.name: No stale vendoring-pattern language on refresh
checks.rule: On REFRESH of an existing AGENTS.md, scan the Active Context, Structure, and Project-specific rules sections for stale vendoring-pattern tokens. If any trigger token describes the CURRENT or RECOMMENDED local posture for the Make-AI-Agents reference (or any other vendored-by-clone resource), the bullet IS the defect — the canonical posture as of 2026-05-13 is `clone+gitignore`, not subtree or submodule. Resolution: rewrite the offending bullet to the canonical clone+gitignore form (template in make_AGENTS.md → Common Pitfall #1), and surface each rewrite in the refresh handoff doc so the maintainer reviews each change explicitly. This is an explicit narrow exception to P-007 (Minimum Diff): minimum-diff does NOT shield language whose factual claim conflicts with the canonical posture — P-001 (Read Before Claiming) takes precedence. PASS: (a) no trigger tokens present in any of the scanned sections; OR (b) trigger tokens appear only as negative-framing (e.g., 'no subtree mechanics in host history', 'Avoid `git subtree` here') or historical-migration notes (e.g., a Migration Notes bullet recording the subtree → clone+gitignore transition). FAIL: a trigger token describes the current local posture as a subtree/submodule. Empirical grounding: three sibling repos (canvas-toolbox, handoff, Make-AI-Agents) each required a hand-rewrite of this defect on 2026-05-13 — that's the signal the template needed the scan rule rather than relying on consumers to hand-fix.

checks.name: Handoff recognition section is structurally complete (and handoff/ co-located when participating)
checks.rule: Working Style contains the canonical handoff recognition block baked from handoff/AGENTS_snippet.md per Quickstart step 4.5. PASS requires ALL of: (a) the section heading `## Handoff document recognition` is present in Working Style; (b) the path-pattern table is present (header row contains `Path pattern` and `What it is`, with at least the rows for `handoffs/HANDOFF_<topic>.md` (outgoing request canonical), `handoffs/<YYYY-MM-DD>_<topic>.md` (incoming deliver canonical), `<CONSUMER>_HANDOFF_<topic>.md` at repo root (incoming request), `<PRODUCER>_DELIVERS_<topic>.md` at repo root (visibility copy), `handoffs/parkinglot.md` (internal near-term), `handoffs/long-term-parking.md` (internal far-horizon)); (c) all 7 numbered rules are present under a `Seven rules` heading or equivalent (rules: Read metadata header first; Act only on Status: delivered; Surface before applying; Update Status on apply; STOP on missing referenced artifacts; Before authoring outbound, read REPO_CARD.md; Do not auto-act on `parked` items); (d) the status-enum quick-lookup table (`## Quick lookup — Status enum`) is present with all values (draft/delivered/applying/applied/archived/superseded/parked); (e) the direction-enum quick-lookup table (`## Quick lookup — Direction enum`) is present with all values (request/deliver/internal). CONDITIONAL SUB-RULE: if the target repo has a `handoffs/` folder (= participates in the convention), then the `handoff/` clone MUST be co-located at the target repo root AND `handoff/` MUST be listed in the target's `.gitignore`. Same propagation pattern as AGENTS-QC-006 for the discipline; if the target does not participate, this sub-rule is N/A. FAIL: any of (a)–(e) missing; OR participation detected but `handoff/` not co-located/ignored. Severity high because without this, generated AGENTS.md files don't recognize handoff documents as a structured artifact lifecycle, and cross-repo coordination silently degrades into prose conversation. Added 2026-05-28 (Sprint F).

conditional_co_location_check.trigger: target repo contains a handoffs/ directory
conditional_co_location_check.requirement: handoff/ clone exists at the target repo root AND handoff/ is listed in the target's .gitignore
conditional_co_location_check.rationale: Mirrors AGENTS-QC-006 condition (b) for the discipline — participation is auto-detectable from the receiving folder; co-location enables the snippet's pointers (handoff/CONVENTION.md, templates/, etc.) to resolve.

changes: Initial bare-bones make_AGENTS skill — sibling to make_agent and make_gem.
Defines required and optional sections for AGENTS.md.
Embeds behavioral discipline pointer (compact_boilerplate.working_style_template) so every generated AGENTS.md inherits the discipline.

5 skill-specific QC checks (AGENTS-QC-001 through AGENTS-QC-005). AGENTS-QC-006 added 2026-05-13 for discipline-file co-location enforcement. AGENTS-QC-007 added 2026-05-14 (issue #10) for refresh-time stale-vendoring-pattern scan — triggered by three same-day hand-fixes across canvas-toolbox, handoff, and Make-AI-Agents after the subtree → clone+gitignore migration on 2026-05-13. AGENTS-QC-008 added 2026-05-28 (Sprint F, Hermes-research synthesis) for handoff convention recognition: every generated AGENTS.md bakes handoff/AGENTS_snippet.md (7-rule canonical) verbatim into Working Style; if the target repo has handoffs/, the handoff/ clone is conditionally co-located + gitignored (same propagation pattern as AGENTS-QC-006 for the discipline). Compact_boilerplate adds handoff_recognition_source pointer + handoff_recognition_instructions; agents_md_template adds {{handoff_recognition_block}}; io_contract.outputs adds conditional handoff_repo_co_located.
Skill itself operates as interaction_pattern: single_write_workflow with applicable_principles P-001, P-002, P-003, P-004, P-006, P-007, P-008, P-009, P-010 (skip P-005).
